title: oreomyrrhis andicola var. colensoi
intro: Oreomyrrhis andicola var. colensoi is a plant species native to New Zealand, often referred to as Colenso's Oreomyrrhis. It is a flowering plant of the carrot family, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine. The plant's roots and leaves are used for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as skin conditions like eczema. Oreomyrrhis andicola var. colensoi can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal benefits, and is also used in topical applications for skin health and wound healing.
